headline: The Spray Foam Crisis: What Property Professionals Need to Know
subheadline: Major lenders now refusing mortgages on spray foam properties
post_text: A growing crisis is hitting the UK property market. Major mortgage lenders — including Halifax, Nationwide, and Santander — are now routinely refusing mortgages on properties with spray foam insulation in the loft. For estate agents, conveyancers, and mortgage brokers, this means one thing: if your client's property has spray foam, the sale WILL stall. We provide safe, professional spray foam removal with full RICS-compliant documentation that satisfies lenders and surveyors. If you're advising clients in England, Wales or Northern Ireland, we can help. Free assessments available.

headline: Millions of UK Homes Have a Hidden Problem
subheadline: Government-funded insulation installed without proper surveys
post_text: Between 2000 and 2023, millions of UK homes received free or subsidised insulation under government schemes like ECO, Green Deal, HHCRO, and Warm Front. The goal was energy efficiency. The reality? Many installations were carried out without proper surveys, on unsuitable properties, by under-qualified installers. The result: penetrating damp, black mould, structural decay, and homeowners left with repair bills of £10,000–£30,000+. If your clients or tenants are affected, they may have a valid claim against the installer, surveyor, and scheme funder. We help homeowners across England, Wales & Northern Ireland assess their eligibility.

headline: Why Damp Complaints Are Really Insulation Complaints
subheadline: The overlooked connection between insulation and moisture damage
post_text: If you manage properties or advise homeowners, you've probably seen a rise in damp and mould complaints over the past decade. In many cases, the root cause isn't ventilation or lifestyle — it's wrongly installed insulation. Cavity wall insulation in unsuitable properties acts as a bridge for moisture. Spray foam in lofts blocks essential ventilation. Both lead to trapped moisture, condensation, and eventually structural damage. We offer free damage assessments for properties in England, Wales & Northern Ireland. If insulation is the cause, the installer may be liable for all remediation costs.

headline: The Financial Impact of Bad Insulation on Property Values
post_text: New data shows that properties with wrongly installed insulation are losing 10–22% of their market value — even before factoring in removal and repair costs. For a £250,000 property, that's £25,000–£55,000 in lost value. Add removal costs (£3,000–£15,000) and structural repairs (£2,000–£5,000), and the total financial impact can exceed £70,000. The good news: if the insulation was installed under a government scheme or without a proper survey, claims of £10,000–£50,000+ are common. We help homeowners and property professionals understand their options.

headline: Insulation Claims: A Briefing for Legal Professionals
subheadline: Time-limited claims against installers and scheme funders
post_text: The insulation claims space is growing rapidly. Key points for solicitors, loss adjustors, and claims management professionals: • Claims arise from negligent installation of cavity wall, loft, and spray foam insulation • Multiple liable parties: installer, surveyor, and scheme funder (energy company) • Many installations under ECO, Green Deal, HHCRO and Warm Front lacked proper pre-installation surveys • Limitation periods apply — and many installer firms are entering insolvency • Typical claim values: £10,000–£50,000+ • Coverage: England, Wales & Northern Ireland (Scottish law differs). We provide expert assessments, removal services, and documentation to support claims. Contact us to discuss referral arrangements.
